zh.lifeSummary: 當Sir John de Brugge於大約1296年出生在英國英格蘭賀福爾布里奇 索萊爾時，他的父親Simon de Brugge II27歲，母親Mary Solers22歲。他於1328年與Sarah在英國英格蘭賀福爾布里奇 索萊爾結婚。他們至少是2個兒子和1個女兒的父母。他去世於1350年在英國賀福爾享年55歲。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Brugge
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Dutch (Van der Brugge): topographic name for someone living near a brug ‘bridge’.
Flemish and Dutch: habitational name from Bruges.
North German (Brügge): variant of Bruck or a habitational name from any of several places called Brügge.

variants.name: Bruck
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Jewish Hyman, Naftali, Eliahu, Hershel, Meyer, Ruvim, Shloime, Yoav.

German (also Brück): topographic name for someone who lived near a bridge, or an occupational name for a bridge keeper or toll collector on a bridge, from Middle High German bruck(e) ‘bridge’. Bruck remained the Bavarian and Austrian form, reflected in placenames such as Innsbruck, whereas in central and northern Germany Brück(e) was the regular and ultimately standard form, as for example in the placename Osnabrück. Switzerland has both forms. See also Brueck .
Jewish (from Poland and Belarus): from Polish, Belorussian, or Yiddish bruk ‘pavement’, possibly a metonymic occupational name for a paver.

nameMeanings.name: John
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Contracted form of Old French Jo(h)anne, from Latin Io(h)anna ( see Joanna ). In England this was the usual feminine form of John from the Middle English period onwards and was extremely popular, but in the 16th and 17th centuries it steadily lost ground to Jane . It was strongly revived in the first part of the 20th century, partly under the influence of George Bernard Shaw's play St Joan ( 1923 ), based on the life of Joan of Arc ( 1412–31 ). Claiming to be guided by the voices of the saints, she persuaded the French dauphin to defy the occupying English forces and have himself crowned, and she led the French army that raised the siege of Orleans in 1429 . The following year she was captured by the Burgundians and sold to the English, and a year later she was burned at the stake for witchcraft at the age of 18 or 19. Her story has captured the imagination of many writers, and she is variously portrayed as a national and political hero, a model of apolitical straightforwardness and honesty, and a religious heroine. She was canonized in 1920 . More recent influences have included the American film actress Joan Crawford ( 1908–77 , born Lucille le Sueur ), the British actress Joan Collins ( b. 1933 ), the American comedienne Joan Rivers ( b. 1933 ), and the West Indian pop singer Joan Armatrading ( b. 1950 ).

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

en.lifeSummary: When Sir John de Brugge was born about 1296, in Bridge Sollers, Herefordshire, England, United Kingdom, his father, Simon de Brugge II, was 27 and his mother, Mary Solers, was 22. He married Sarah in 1328, in Bridge Sollers, Herefordshire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 2 sons and 1 daughter. He died in 1350, in Herefordshire, England, at the age of 55.

Il a épousé Sarah en 1328, à Bridge Sollers, Herefordshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils et 1 fille. Il est décédé en 1350, à Herefordshire, Angleterre, à l’âge de 55 ans.

ko.lifeSummary: Sir John de Brugge(이)가 대략 1296년 영국 잉글랜드 허퍼드실르 브리지 솔레르스에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Simon de Brugge II(은)는 27세였고, 그의 어머니 Mary Solers(은)는 22세였다. 그는 1328년 영국 잉글랜드 허퍼드실르 브리지 솔레르스에서 Sarah(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1350년 영국 허퍼드실르에서 55세의 나이로 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Sir John de Brugge родился около 1296 г. в местности бридж соллирс, хирефордшир, Англия, Великобритания, его отцу по имени Simon de Brugge II было 27 лет, а матери, которую звали Mary Solers, 22. Он женился на женщине по имени Sarah в 1328 г. в местности бридж соллирс, хирефордшир, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына и 1 дочь. Он умер в 1350 г. в местности хирефордшир, Англия в возрасте 55 лет.
